title: Cheap Houses for Rent in Collinwood, Cleveland
metaTitle: Cheap Houses for Rent in Collinwood, Cleveland OH
metaDescription: Cheap houses for rent in Collinwood, Cleveland (44110) — budget 2- and 3-bedroom homes from a local, voucher-friendly property manager. Book a free showing.
primaryKeyword: cheap houses for rent Collinwood cleveland
answerBox: Collinwood, in Cleveland's ZIP code 44110, is one of the city's lower-cost neighborhoods for a rental house. Two-bedroom homes generally start around $750/month and three-bedroom homes around $1,000/month, below Cleveland's citywide median, and every home we manage in the area accepts Housing Choice Vouchers.

sections.h2: Where to find a cheap house to rent in Collinwood
body: The most budget-friendly rental houses in Collinwood sit within **ZIP code 44110**, on Cleveland's far northeast side, split between the inland Collinwood-Nottingham blocks and the North Shore Collinwood area closer to Lake Erie. Because the housing stock is largely early-1900s single-family homes and duplexes rather than newer construction, asking rents here tend to run below what larger, professionally-managed apartment buildings charge citywide.

Collinwood is also one of the ZIP codes where our portfolio has the most homes, so it's a practical place to start a budget search. We manage voucher-friendly rental homes throughout the area — [tell us your budget and bedroom count](/p/book-showing) and our team will follow up with what's currently available.

On the lower end, two-bedroom houses and duplex units in 44110 can be found from about $750 a month, with most falling somewhere between $750 and $1,000 depending on condition, size, and whether utilities are included in the rent. Three-bedroom homes stretch a bit higher, generally $1,000 to $1,300, but even the top of that range sits below Cleveland's overall median asking rent.

For context, Zumper's July 4, 2026 report puts Cleveland's citywide median at **$1,250/month**, with 2-bedrooms averaging $1,100 and 3-bedrooms averaging $1,350 — Collinwood's budget-tier houses generally undercut both figures. RentCafe's July 2, 2026 report on larger managed apartment buildings shows an even higher citywide average of **$1,818** for a 2-bedroom unit, which reflects newer complexes rather than the older individually-owned houses that make up most of Collinwood's rental stock.

sections.h2: What keeps rent low in this part of Cleveland
body: Collinwood's housing stock is dominated by older, individually-owned single-family homes and duplexes rather than large apartment complexes, which is the main reason prices trend below citywide averages. Renters looking to stay on the lower end of the range should ask upfront whether heat, water, and electric are included, since an all-in monthly cost can shift the real comparison between two listings that look similar on paper.

The neighborhood is served by RTA bus routes into downtown and sits near the Red Line's Windermere station, the eastern end of the heavy-rail line that also reaches Cleveland Hopkins Airport, which can reduce the need for a car and its associated costs. Waterloo Road's Waterloo Arts District puts a commercial strip for everyday errands within a few blocks of many Collinwood addresses, and I-90 (the Lakeland Freeway) is a short drive from most Collinwood streets for renters who do drive.

items: **Transit:** RTA bus lines and the Red Line's Windermere terminus nearby
**Commercial corridor:** Waterloo Road / Waterloo Arts District
**Highway access:** I-90 (Lakeland Freeway) a short drive from most streets

sections.h2: Section 8 and voucher-friendly homes in Collinwood
body: Every home we manage in Collinwood accepts Housing Choice Vouchers (Section 8) and is HUD-inspection-ready, which can make a voucher a useful tool for keeping your monthly out-of-pocket rent low. Keep in mind that Ohio has no statewide source-of-income protection law, and the City of Cleveland does not currently require landlords to accept vouchers — only a handful of Cuyahoga County suburbs, including Cleveland Heights, South Euclid, University Heights, Warrensville Heights, and Linndale, have local ordinances doing so. Apply for a voucher through [CMHA](https://www.cmha.net), and see our [Cleveland Section 8 guide](/section-8-housing-cleveland-oh/) for the full application process.

For a broader look at rental houses across the city, including neighborhoods outside Collinwood, see our [houses for rent in Cleveland](/houses-for-rent-cleveland-oh/) pillar page or the [full Collinwood neighborhood guide](/cleveland-rentals/neighborhoods/houses-for-rent-collinwood-cleveland/).

faq.q: What's the cheapest a house rents for in Collinwood?
faq.a: The lowest-priced two-bedroom houses and duplex units in Collinwood (ZIP 44110) start around $750 a month, with most falling between $750 and $1,000. Three-bedroom homes generally run $1,000 to $1,300. Exact pricing depends on condition, size, and whether utilities are included.

faq.q: Why is Collinwood cheaper than other Cleveland neighborhoods?
faq.a: Collinwood's rental stock is mostly older, individually-owned single-family homes and duplexes rather than newer apartment complexes, which keeps asking rents below Cleveland's citywide median of $1,250 reported by Zumper in July 2026 and well below professionally-managed building averages.

faq.q: Are there Section 8 houses for rent in Collinwood?
faq.a: Yes. Every home we manage in Collinwood accepts Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready. Collinwood (44110) is one of the ZIP codes where we manage the most rental homes, so it's a practical place to start a voucher-friendly search. Book a showing for current availability.

faq.q: What ZIP code should I search for cheap houses in Collinwood?
faq.a: Search ZIP code 44110, which covers both the North Shore Collinwood lakefront area and the inland Collinwood-Nottingham blocks on Cleveland's far northeast side.

faq.q: Will renting cheap in Collinwood mean a smaller home?
faq.a: Not necessarily. Budget-tier pricing in Collinwood reflects the age and ownership structure of the housing stock more than square footage — many lower-cost listings are full two- or three-bedroom houses with a basement and yard, not studio or efficiency units.
